Prompt
U
Oracle Events
1. Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "outreach" or "Q1 Outreach" or "pipeline" or similar) to find David Chen's Q1 Outreach Objectives post in #customer-engagement (C002) from January 5, 2026. Expected findings: each AM assigned territory focus, with Mina Hashimoto on West Coast tech startups, Catalina Dubois on Midwest enterprise accounts, Emeka Diallo on Southeast relationship-oriented accounts. Target: at least 4 cold/warm outreach emails per AM by end of week 1, pipeline value $150K+ per AM, close rate goal 40%+.
2. Search emails using search_emails (query: "outreach" or "prospect" or "interest" or "relocation services" or similar) to find outbound cold outreach emails from Mina, Catalina, and Emeka to various prospect companies, and any inbound replies expressing interest. Expected findings: replies from at least 6 companies expressing interest, including Driftwood Analytics, Harbour Pharmaceutical, Foxglove Biotech, Meridian Genomics, Summit Health Systems, and Prism Communications.
3. Search CRM leads using crm_search_leads (various queries by company name, or browsing all leads) to find all cold outreach leads and their current status. Expected findings: 6 leads with warm or hot rating: (1) Driftwood Analytics / Rachel Huang (warm, "Replied with interest, asked about pricing tiers"), (2) Harbour Pharmaceutical / Claire Fontaine (hot, "Replied requesting assistance with one specific relocation, urgent timeline"), (3) Foxglove Biotech / Naomi Tran (warm, "Replied asking about temporary housing options"), (4) Meridian Genomics / James Osei (warm, "Replied asking about pricing tiers and contract minimums"), (5) Summit Health Systems / Kevin Sato (warm, "Replied asking whether MoveOps services include home-office equipment stipend management"), (6) Prism Communications / Brianna Cole (warm, "Replied asking specifically about pet relocation and furniture shipping coverage").
4. Search CRM deals using crm_search_deals (query: "Driftwood" or "Harbour" or "Foxglove" or "Meridian" or "Summit Health" or "Prism" or similar) to determine which interested prospects were converted to deals. Expected findings: Driftwood Analytics ($15,000, appointmentscheduled), Harbour Pharmaceutical ($4,800, qualifiedtobuy), and Foxglove Biotech ($5,800, qualifiedtobuy) have deals. Meridian Genomics, Summit Health Systems, and Prism Communications have NO deals created; these are the dropped prospects.
5. Search Airtable client accounts using airtable_search_records (base_id: discovered via airtable_list_bases, table_name: "Client Accounts", field_name: "Status", value: "Prospect" or similar) or airtable_list_records to find the dropped prospects' client account records. Expected findings: (1) Meridian Genomics, Account Manager: Mina Hashimoto, Status: Prospect, Notes: "Awaiting follow-up"; (2) Summit Health Systems, Account Manager: Catalina Dubois, Status: Prospect, Notes: "Awaiting follow-up"; (3) Prism Communications, Account Manager: Emeka Diallo, Status: Prospect, Notes: "Awaiting follow-up". All three show zero total relocations and no contract type. This confirms which AM was responsible for each dropped lead.
6. Search emails using search_emails (query: "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "invoice" or "wrong" or similar) to find the invoice swap chain. Expected findings: (1) Hana Kim's April 10 batch send of invoices to Raj Greenfield (Sunbelt, $4,800) and Carmen Delgado-Reyes (Palmetto, $2,400); (2) Raj Greenfield's complaint that the PDF header showed "Palmetto Community Foundation" and the total was $2,400 instead of $4,800; (3) Carmen Delgado-Reyes's complaint that the attached document showed $4,800, roughly double the expected Palmetto amount; (4) Raj Greenfield's escalation to David Chen asking about nonprofit pricing; (5) Victoria Chen-Nakamura (COO, Sunbelt) escalating to demand portfolio-wide pricing discussion; (6) Emeka Diallo's corrective emails sent to both clients.
7. Search QuickBooks for Sunbelt and Palmetto invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria filtering by customer name "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or similar). Expected findings: (1) INV-2026-0398, Sunbelt Venture Partners, $4,800, April 2026 relocation services, due 2026-05-10; (2) INV-2026-0399, Palmetto Community Foundation, $2,400 (nonprofit rate), April 2026 relocation support, due 2026-05-10; plus historical invoices: INV-2026-005 ($14,000, 2 Denver relocations), INV-2026-013 ($7,000, repeat Chicago to Miami), INV-2026-006 ($3,200, 1 nonprofit Atlanta to Raleigh). Confirms QuickBooks records were correct; the failure was in the email attachment layer, not the accounting records.
8. Search CRM for Sunbelt and Palmetto companies using crm_search_companies (name: "Sunbelt") and crm_search_companies (name: "Palmetto"). Expected findings: company_sunbelt, Customer, Miami FL, FinTech, description: "Strong relationship with Emeka"; crm_company_69f1ae00a4b9, Palmetto Community Foundation, customer, Charleston SC, Nonprofit, "reduced-rate billing and audit-sensitive documentation requirements." Confirms Emeka manages both accounts.
9. Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "invoice swap" or "pricing exposure" or similar) to find internal discussions about the incident. Expected findings: Emeka's reconstruction confirming swapped PDF attachments; Marcus Thorne's confirmation that QuickBooks records are correct but PDFs exposed two different pricing regimes; Samira Tariq's mention of a dormant attachment-mismatch safeguard ticket resurfaced; David Chen's framing of this as a "second pricing-exposure incident" after the Terraform reply-all.
10. Search emails using search_emails (query: "NorthWind" or "Pam Kowalski" or "final warning" or "PathFinder" or "retention" or similar) to find the formal escalation. Expected findings: Pam Kowalski's email to David Chen (CC: Victor Huang, CISO) stating "Please treat this as a formal escalation and final warning before we decide whether to move this work elsewhere." She cites two incidents: (1) Raj Patel server lab relocation, a chain-of-custody breach (loaded before IT escort, overnight warehouse storage); (2) Emilia Cruz piano damage, a scratched Steinway during extraction. Pam explicitly mentions "preliminary conversations with PathFinder Logistics regarding Denver-area relocation support." Demands: corrective action plan, Emilia Cruz resolution/reimbursement, named account coverage, commercial consideration. Pam bypasses Catalina Dubois: "I do not think routing this back through her is appropriate."
11. Search CRM for NorthWind using crm_search_companies (name: "NorthWind") and crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_northwind" or dealname: "NorthWind" or similar). Expected findings: company_northwind, Customer, Chicago, Manufacturing Tech, "Catalina's account." CRM deals include: (1) original $60,000 contract (5 enterprise relocations, closedWon), (2) Denver expansion deal $14,700 (2 relocations, qualifiedToBuy or contractSent), with notes: "Pam Kowalski explicitly stated this is a second chance" and "ACCOUNT RECOVERY: NorthWind Q1 CSAT was 5/10."
12. Search CRM engagements for NorthWind using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_northwind"]) to find the account recovery context. Expected findings: engagement documenting Q1 execution failures, Pam's "second chance" statement, and Catalina's acknowledged slow response times.
13. Search Linear for NorthWind retention tracking using linear_list_issues (query: "NorthWind" or "retention" or similar). Expected finding: Issue #482, "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations", priority 1 (urgent), assigned to David Chen (moveops_david_chen), due 2026-04-24, labels: ["northwind", "retention-risk", "exec-visible"]. Description references: Pam's final warning, Victor Huang security breach, chain-of-custody failures, Catalina's broken April 14 promise, and framing as "save-or-lose moment."
14. Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "NorthWind" or "Pam" or "PathFinder" or "retention" or similar) to find internal discussions about the churn crisis. Expected findings: David Chen's assessment of the situation, discussion about whether to redistribute Catalina's accounts, Elena's directive to "handle it within the team," and commentary on PathFinder as competitor threat.
15. Search emails using search_emails (query: "Ridgeline" or "referral" or "Kenji Watanabe" or "Anya Petrova" or similar) to find the referral chain. Expected findings: (1) Anya Petrova (CEO, StormCloud Analytics, anya.petrova@stormcloud.io) sent warm referral email on April 18 introducing Kenji Watanabe (HR Director, Ridgeline Robotics) to MoveOps for an Austin relocation batch of 5 employees; CC'd mina.hashimoto@moveops.com and kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com; (2) Kenji Watanabe sent a follow-up email on April 21, still marked unread (is_read: false); (3) David Chen's belated recovery email to Kenji on April 24; (4) Mina Hashimoto's introduction email to Kenji on April 24; (5) Liam Park (StormCloud CFO) escalation email on April 25 mentioning "Anya made a warm introduction to Ridgeline Robotics and I am now hearing they have not gotten a response."
16. Search CRM for Ridgeline Robotics using crm_search_companies (name: "Ridgeline") and crm_search_deals (dealname: "Ridgeline" or similar) and crm_search_leads (full_name: "Kenji Watanabe" or similar). Expected findings: (1) crm_company_d07c0ec27cf2, Prospect, Austin TX, Robotics, 180 employees, "Warm referral from StormCloud Analytics for an Austin relocation batch of five employees", created 2026-04-24; (2) CRM deal "Ridgeline Robotics - Austin Relocation Batch (5 employees)", $8,750, stage: Qualified To Buy, created 2026-04-24, description: "Initial outreach from Kenji Watanabe went unanswered for several days before recovery outreach on 2026-04-24"; (3) CRM lead for Kenji Watanabe, HR Director, leadsource: Referral, rating: Hot.
17. Search CRM for StormCloud Analytics using crm_search_companies (name: "StormCloud") to confirm referral came from an existing client. Expected finding: company_stormcloud, type: Customer, Denver, SaaS/Analytics, "Good relationship with Mina." Confirms StormCloud is an active MoveOps client, making this a referral from an existing customer.
18. Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Ridgeline" or "referral" or "Anya" or "Kenji" or similar) to find internal discussion about the dropped referral. Expected findings: (1) David Chen's April 19 celebratory post in #customer-engagement: "Anya just sent over a warm intro to Kenji Watanabe at Ridgeline Robotics for an Austin batch of 5"; (2) Mina's April 25 challenge: "I just checked the StormCloud referral chain...right now it looks untouched"; (3) David's April 25 admission: "this is on me. I celebrated Anya's referral...but we never turned it into a live deal, and Kenji's 4/21 follow-up is still sitting unanswered in my inbox."
19. Look up Elena Rostova's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Elena Rostova" or similar). Expected finding: elena.rostova@moveops.com.
20. Look up David Chen's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "David Chen" or similar). Expected finding: david.chen@moveops.com.
21. List Slack channels using channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel") to identify the #executive channel ID. Expected finding: C004, the "executive" channel with purpose "Leadership strategy, company direction, and high-level decision making."
22. Send briefing email using send_email (sender: "priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com", recipients: ["elena.rostova@moveops.com"]) with detailed findings across all four threads, including:
(a) Cold outreach: 6 prospects responded with interest, 3 converted to deals (Driftwood $15K, Harbour $4.8K, Foxglove $5.8K), 3 completely dropped (Meridian Genomics by Mina, Summit Health by Catalina, Prism Communications by Emeka), all still "Awaiting follow-up" with no deal created;
(b) Invoice swap: Sunbelt Venture Partners ($4,800, INV-2026-0398) and Palmetto Community Foundation ($2,400, INV-2026-0399) received each other's PDF attachments on April 10; Sunbelt saw Palmetto's nonprofit rate; Victoria Chen-Nakamura (Sunbelt COO) demanding portfolio-wide pricing; corrective invoices sent by Emeka but commercial damage ongoing;
(c) NorthWind: Pam Kowalski's formal final warning received; PathFinder Logistics in preliminary conversations; retention plan (Linear #482) assigned to David Chen; Catalina's April 14 recovery promise unfulfilled;
(d) Dropped referral: Ridgeline Robotics (5 Austin employees, $8,750 potential) referred by StormCloud's Anya Petrova on April 18; went unanswered for 6 days; belated recovery on April 24 by David and Mina.
Email must include real financial figures from QuickBooks, not vague summaries.
23. Post high-level summary using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C004") in the #executive Slack channel. Content should cover all four situations at a summary level for the leadership team (David, Marcus, Chloe). Must NOT include sensitive financial details such as specific invoice amounts, nonprofit pricing differentials, or client-specific billing data; those go in Elena's email only.
24. Create CRM engagement notes using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE") on relevant accounts:
(1) On Sunbelt Venture Partners (company_ids: ["company_sunbelt"]), documenting the invoice swap incident, pricing exposure, Victoria Chen-Nakamura's escalation, and current remediation status;
(2) On Palmetto Community Foundation (company_ids: ["crm_company_69f1ae00a4b9"]), documenting the invoice swap from Palmetto's side, audit-sensitivity concerns from Carmen Delgado-Reyes;
(3) On NorthWind Technologies (company_ids: ["company_northwind"]), documenting the final warning status, PathFinder threat, retention plan progress;
(4) On Ridgeline Robotics or StormCloud Analytics (company_ids: ["crm_company_d07c0ec27cf2"] or ["company_stormcloud"]), documenting the dropped referral status and recovery actions taken.
25. Create Linear tracking tickets using linear_create_issue for untracked items:
(1) Cold outreach pipeline recovery, title related to "Cold Outreach Pipeline Gap: 3 Dropped Prospects Requiring Follow-Up" (or similar), description listing Meridian Genomics, Summit Health Systems, and Prism Communications with their contact names, interest signals, and assigned AMs. Team: relevant team, priority: 2 (high);
(2) Sunbelt/Palmetto commercial fallout, check Linear for existing tracking (the attachment-mismatch safeguard ticket is engineering-focused). If no client-facing incident ticket exists, create one titled "Sunbelt/Palmetto Invoice Swap: Client Pricing Exposure Remediation" (or similar) tracking the commercial response to Victoria Chen-Nakamura's portfolio pricing demand.
26. Schedule pipeline review call using calendar_add_calendar_event (title: "Pipeline Review: Elena + David" or similar, start_datetime: April 28, 2026 or April 29, 2026 (early next week from April 26), attendees: ["elena.rostova@moveops.com", "david.chen@moveops.com"], description referencing cold outreach gaps, at-risk accounts, and referral follow-up).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

Oracle Events
1. Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "outreach" or "Q1 Outreach" or "pipeline" or similar) to find David Chen's Q1 Outreach Objectives post in #customer-engagement (C002) from January 5, 2026. Expected findings: each AM assigned territory focus, with Mina Hashimoto on West Coast tech startups, Catalina Dubois on Midwest enterprise accounts, Emeka Diallo on Southeast relationship-oriented accounts. Target: at least 4 cold/warm outreach emails per AM by end of week 1, pipeline value $150K+ per AM, close rate goal 40%+.
2. Search emails using search_emails (query: "outreach" or "prospect" or "interest" or "relocation services" or similar) to find outbound cold outreach emails from Mina, Catalina, and Emeka to various prospect companies, and any inbound replies expressing interest. Expected findings: replies from at least 6 companies expressing interest, including Driftwood Analytics, Harbour Pharmaceutical, Foxglove Biotech, Meridian Genomics, Summit Health Systems, and Prism Communications.
3. Search CRM leads using crm_search_leads (various queries by company name, or browsing all leads) to find all cold outreach leads and their current status. Expected findings: 6 leads with warm or hot rating: (1) Driftwood Analytics / Rachel Huang (warm, "Replied with interest, asked about pricing tiers"), (2) Harbour Pharmaceutical / Claire Fontaine (hot, "Replied requesting assistance with one specific relocation, urgent timeline"), (3) Foxglove Biotech / Naomi Tran (warm, "Replied asking about temporary housing options"), (4) Meridian Genomics / James Osei (warm, "Replied asking about pricing tiers and contract minimums"), (5) Summit Health Systems / Kevin Sato (warm, "Replied asking whether MoveOps services include home-office equipment stipend management"), (6) Prism Communications / Brianna Cole (warm, "Replied asking specifically about pet relocation and furniture shipping coverage").
4. Search CRM deals using crm_search_deals (query: "Driftwood" or "Harbour" or "Foxglove" or "Meridian" or "Summit Health" or "Prism" or similar) to determine which interested prospects were converted to deals. Expected findings: Driftwood Analytics ($15,000, appointmentscheduled), Harbour Pharmaceutical ($4,800, qualifiedtobuy), and Foxglove Biotech ($5,800, qualifiedtobuy) have deals. Meridian Genomics, Summit Health Systems, and Prism Communications have NO deals created; these are the dropped prospects.
5. Search Airtable client accounts using airtable_search_records (base_id: discovered via airtable_list_bases, table_name: "Client Accounts", field_name: "Status", value: "Prospect" or similar) or airtable_list_records to find the dropped prospects' client account records. Expected findings: (1) Meridian Genomics, Account Manager: Mina Hashimoto, Status: Prospect, Notes: "Awaiting follow-up"; (2) Summit Health Systems, Account Manager: Catalina Dubois, Status: Prospect, Notes: "Awaiting follow-up"; (3) Prism Communications, Account Manager: Emeka Diallo, Status: Prospect, Notes: "Awaiting follow-up". All three show zero total relocations and no contract type. This confirms which AM was responsible for each dropped lead.
6. Search emails using search_emails (query: "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "invoice" or "wrong" or similar) to find the invoice swap chain. 10. Search emails using search_emails (query: "NorthWind" or "Pam Kowalski" or "final warning" or "PathFinder" or "retention" or similar) to find the formal escalation. She cites two incidents: (1) Raj Patel server lab relocation, a chain-of-custody breach (loaded before IT escort, overnight warehouse storage); (2) Emilia Cruz piano damage, a scratched Steinway during extraction. Pam bypasses Catalina Dubois: "I do not think routing this back through her is appropriate."
11. Search CRM for NorthWind using crm_search_companies (name: "NorthWind") and crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_northwind" or dealname: "NorthWind" or similar). Expected findings: company_northwind, Customer, Chicago, Manufacturing Tech, "Catalina's account." CRM deals include: (1) original $60,000 contract (5 enterprise relocations, closedWon), (2) Denver expansion deal $14,700 (2 relocations, qualifiedToBuy or contractSent), with notes: "Pam Kowalski explicitly stated this is a second chance" and "ACCOUNT RECOVERY: NorthWind Q1 CSAT was 5/10."
12. Search CRM engagements for NorthWind using crm_list_engagements (company_ids: ["company_northwind"]) to find the account recovery context. Expected findings: engagement documenting Q1 execution failures, Pam's "second chance" statement, and Catalina's acknowledged slow response times.
13. Search Linear for NorthWind retention tracking using linear_list_issues (query: "NorthWind" or "retention" or similar). Expected finding: Issue #482, "NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations", priority 1 (urgent), assigned to David Chen (moveops_david_chen), due 2026-04-24, labels: ["northwind", "retention-risk", "exec-visible"]. Description references: Pam's final warning, Victor Huang security breach, chain-of-custody failures, Catalina's broken April 14 promise, and framing as "save-or-lose moment."
14. Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "NorthWind" or "Pam" or "PathFinder" or "retention" or similar) to find internal discussions about the churn crisis. Expected findings: David Chen's assessment of the situation, discussion about whether to redistribute Catalina's accounts, Elena's directive to "handle it within the team," and commentary on PathFinder as competitor threat.
15. Search emails using search_emails (query: "Ridgeline" or "referral" or "Kenji Watanabe" or "Anya Petrova" or similar) to find the referral chain. Expected findings: (1) Anya Petrova (CEO, StormCloud Analytics, anya.petrova@stormcloud.io) sent warm referral email on April 18 introducing Kenji Watanabe (HR Director, Ridgeline Robotics) to MoveOps for an Austin relocation batch of 5 employees; CC'd mina.hashimoto@moveops.com and kenji.watanabe@ridgelinerobotics.com; (2) Kenji Watanabe sent a follow-up email on April 21, still marked unread (is_read: false); (3) David Chen's belated recovery email to Kenji on April 24; (4) Mina Hashimoto's introduction email to Kenji on April 24; (5) Liam Park (StormCloud CFO) escalation email on April 25 mentioning "Anya made a warm introduction to Ridgeline Robotics and I am now hearing they have not gotten a response."
16. Search CRM for Ridgeline Robotics using crm_search_companies (name: "Ridgeline") and crm_search_deals (dealname: "Ridgeline" or similar) and crm_search_leads (full_name: "Kenji Watanabe" or similar). Expected findings: (1) crm_company_d07c0ec27cf2, Prospect, Austin TX, Robotics, 180 employees, "Warm referral from StormCloud Analytics for an Austin relocation batch of five employees", created 2026-04-24; (2) CRM deal "Ridgeline Robotics - Austin Relocation Batch (5 employees)", $8,750, stage: Qualified To Buy, created 2026-04-24, description: "Initial outreach from Kenji Watanabe went unanswered for several days before recovery outreach on 2026-04-24"; (3) CRM lead for Kenji Watanabe, HR Director, leadsource: Referral, rating: Hot.
17. Search CRM for StormCloud Analytics using crm_search_companies (name: "StormCloud") to confirm referral came from an existing client. Expected finding: company_stormcloud, type: Customer, Denver, SaaS/Analytics, "Good relationship with Mina." Confirms StormCloud is an active MoveOps client, making this a referral from an existing customer.
18. Search Slack using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Ridgeline" or "referral" or "Anya" or "Kenji" or similar) to find internal discussion about the dropped referral. Expected findings: (1) David Chen's April 19 celebratory post in #customer-engagement: "Anya just sent over a warm intro to Kenji Watanabe at Ridgeline Robotics for an Austin batch of 5"; (2) Mina's April 25 challenge: "I just checked the StormCloud referral chain...right now it looks untouched"; (3) David's April 25 admission: "this is on me. I celebrated Anya's referral...but we never turned it into a live deal, and Kenji's 4/21 follow-up is still sitting unanswered in my inbox."
19. Look up Elena Rostova's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Elena Rostova" or similar). Expected finding: elena.rostova@moveops.com.
20. Look up David Chen's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "David Chen" or similar). Expected finding: david.chen@moveops.com.
21. List Slack channels using channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel") to identify the #executive channel ID. Expected finding: C004, the "executive" channel with purpose "Leadership strategy, company direction, and high-level decision making."
22. Send briefing email using send_email (sender: "priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com", recipients: ["elena.rostova@moveops.com"]) with detailed findings across all four threads, including:
(a) Cold outreach: 6 prospects responded with interest, 3 converted to deals (Driftwood $15K, Harbour $4.8K, Foxglove $5.8K), 3 completely dropped (Meridian Genomics by Mina, Summit Health by Catalina, Prism Communications by Emeka), all still "Awaiting follow-up" with no deal created;
(b) Invoice swap: Sunbelt Venture Partners ($4,800, INV-2026-0398) and Palmetto Community Foundation ($2,400, INV-2026-0399) received each other's PDF attachments on April 10; Sunbelt saw Palmetto's nonprofit rate; Victoria Chen-Nakamura (Sunbelt COO) demanding portfolio-wide pricing; corrective invoices sent by Emeka but commercial damage ongoing;
(c) NorthWind: Pam Kowalski's formal final warning received; PathFinder Logistics in preliminary conversations; retention plan (Linear #482) assigned to David Chen; Catalina's April 14 recovery promise unfulfilled;
(d) Dropped referral: Ridgeline Robotics (5 Austin employees, $8,750 potential) referred by StormCloud's Anya Petrova on April 18; went unanswered for 6 days; belated recovery on April 24 by David and Mina.
Email must include real financial figures from QuickBooks, not vague summaries.
23. Post high-level summary using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C004") in the #executive Slack channel. Content should cover all four situations at a summary level for the leadership team (David, Marcus, Chloe). Must NOT include sensitive financial details such as specific invoice amounts, nonprofit pricing differentials, or client-specific billing data; those go in Elena's email only.
24. Create CRM engagement notes using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "NOTE") on relevant accounts:
(1) On Sunbelt Venture Partners (company_ids: ["company_sunbelt"]), documenting the invoice swap incident, pricing exposure, Victoria Chen-Nakamura's escalation, and current remediation status;
(2) On Palmetto Community Foundation (company_ids: ["crm_company_69f1ae00a4b9"]), documenting the invoice swap from Palmetto's side, audit-sensitivity concerns from Carmen Delgado-Reyes;
(3) On NorthWind Technologies (company_ids: ["company_northwind"]), documenting the final warning status, PathFinder threat, retention plan progress;
(4) On Ridgeline Robotics or StormCloud Analytics (company_ids: ["crm_company_d07c0ec27cf2"] or ["company_stormcloud"]), documenting the dropped referral status and recovery actions taken.
25. Create Linear tracking tickets using linear_create_issue for untracked items:
(1) Cold outreach pipeline recovery, title related to "Cold Outreach Pipeline Gap: 3 Dropped Prospects Requiring Follow-Up" (or similar), description listing Meridian Genomics, Summit Health Systems, and Prism Communications with their contact names, interest signals, and assigned AMs. Team: relevant team, priority: 2 (high);
(2) Sunbelt/Palmetto commercial fallout, check Linear for existing tracking (the attachment-mismatch safeguard ticket is engineering-focused). If no client-facing incident ticket exists, create one titled "Sunbelt/Palmetto Invoice Swap: Client Pricing Exposure Remediation" (or similar) tracking the commercial response to Victoria Chen-Nakamura's portfolio pricing demand.
26. Schedule pipeline review call using calendar_add_calendar_event (title: "Pipeline Review: Elena + David" or similar, start_datetime: April 28, 2026 or April 29, 2026 (early next week from April 26), attendees: ["elena.rostova@moveops.com", "david.chen@moveops.com"], description referencing cold outreach gaps, at-risk accounts, and referral follow-up).

✗Run #3The calendar event was scheduled for 2026-04-27 10:00:00 (April 27), but the criteria requires April 28 or April 29. Per the rubric, the universe date is April 26, 2026 (Sunday) and 'early next week' resolves to Monday April 28 or Tuesday April 29. The agent used April 27 instead.
✗Run #4The calendar event was scheduled for April 27, 2026, but the criteria specifies April 28 or April 29 as valid dates for 'early next week' given the universe date of April 26, 2026 (Sunday). April 27 is a Monday and would be the first business day, but the criteria explicitly requires April 28 or April 29. The agent missed this by one day.
✗Run #5The calendar event was scheduled for 2026-04-27 (April 27), not April 28 or April 29. Per the criteria, with a universe date of April 26 (Sunday), early next week resolves to Monday April 28 or Tuesday April 29. April 27 does not satisfy this requirement.
